dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires inverse volatility-based exposure adjustment. Used hvol_20 (20-period historical volatility) as the realized volatility metric. Entry conditions trigger exposure increases when volatility is low (<0.01 or 1%) and exposure decreases when volatility is high (>0.03 or 3%). Exit conditions trigger rebalancing when volatility crosses key thresholds (±0.02 or 2%), simulating regime changes. Rebalancing frequency set to real-time via hold_days_max=0. Applied to both long and short directions per BOTH specification.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires inverse volatility-based exposure adjustment. Used hvol_20 (20-period historical volatility) as the realized volatility metric. Entry conditions trigger exposure increases when volatility is low (<0.01 or 1%) and exposure decreases when volatility is high (>0.03 or 3%). Exit conditions trigger rebalancing when volatility crosses key thresholds (±0.02 or 2%), simulating regime changes. Rebalancing frequency set to real-time via hold_days_max=0. Applied to both long and short directions per BOTH specification.

source_rule_text: Adjust portfolio risk exposure inversely to realized volatility: decrease exposure when volatility is high, increase exposure when volatility is low
